findings.title: Tracker::processBatch never got the backlog-#15 in-tracker time sort: an unsorted fixed-rate batch makes arrival-order front() the scan instant, silently stale-dropping whole batches of fresh data or letting stale members bypass the guard
findings.description: Backlog #15 (docs/algorithms/improvement-backlog.md:561-612) diagnosed this exact footgun — 'front().time is the wrong instant and with reject_stale_measurements on the out-of-order tail is silently dropped' — restated the contract as 'a batch may arrive in any order; the tracker orders it', and shipped the fix as an is_sorted check + stable_sort in MhtTracker::processBatch (MhtTracker.cpp:216-226) and PmbmTracker::processBatch (PmbmTracker.cpp:1323-1333). The third processBatch implementation, the single-hypothesis Tracker::processBatch (Tracker.cpp:195-206), was never updated: it takes t = scan_in.front().time (line 197) from the raw arrival order, gates the WHOLE batch on that one element against high_water_ (lines 198-206, dropping scan_in.size() measurements when the front is stale), calls manager_.predictAll(estimator_, t) with t possibly earlier than in-batch measurements (line 218), and stamps noteObservation(id, t) rather than the matched measurement's own time (lines 273, 305). The backlog entry only says 'Confirmed present in BOTH MhtTracker and PmbmTracker' — plain Tracker was apparently never checked. tests/pipeline/test_batch_ordering.cpp covers only MhtTracker and PmbmTracker (no navtracker::Tracker case), so nothing pins the gap. The integration guide's remote-track recipe (docs/integration-guide.md:353, tracker.processBatch(remote.poll())) hands a poll()-collected batch straight to processBatch with no sort-first warning, and CLAUDE.md's pluggable-hot-path invariant promises the same pipeline works across associator choices — a consumer moving from PmbmTracker/MhtTracker (which tolerate unsorted batches) to Tracker+GNN with identical middleware silently regresses. Tracker.hpp:37 still carries the pre-#15 'share a common timestamp' wording, which is now inconsistent with the restated cross-tracker contract.
findings.failure_scenario: Consumer runs the canonical fixed-rate loop (collect everything since the last tick, hand it over — the pattern MhtTracker.cpp:209-211 names as canonical) against Tracker+GNN, fusing ARPA (≈0.05 s latency) with AIS (0.5-1.0 s latency, the repo's own defaultMaritimeSkewProfile numbers). Tick 1 delivers [radar z@t=9.95]; high_water_=9.95. Tick 2 delivers only the delayed AIS fix [z@t=9.60] (arrived after the radar report but timestamped earlier): front=9.60 < high_water_ → with reject_stale_ default ON the ENTIRE batch is dropped and only counted in stale_dropped_ — the AIS stream is repeatedly discarded whenever its stamp lags the last radar stamp, i.e. most of the time, so AIS effectively never fuses. Conversely a mixed tick [radar z@t=10.55, ais z@t=9.90] passes the guard (front fresh), predictAll advances every track to 10.55, then the AIS match updates against the 10.55 state and rewinds track.last_update to 9.90, so the next predict re-propagates the 0.65 s overlap through F and Q a second time — the exact double-propagation failure the stale guard's own doc (Tracker.hpp:74-81) says the guard exists to prevent. Both failure modes are silent (no sink, no error), and neither occurs with MhtTracker/PmbmTracker on the same input.
findings.evidence: Tracker.cpp:195-206 (no sort, t = scan_in.front().time, whole-batch stale drop 'stale_dropped_ += scan_in.size()'), 218 (predictAll to front time), 273/305 (noteObservation(id, t)). Contrast MhtTracker.cpp:216-226 and PmbmTracker.cpp:1323-1333 (is_sorted + stable_sort with the comment 'Order it by time here so the caller need not: otherwise scan.front().time is the wrong instant and, with the stale guard on, a front older than the high-water mark drops the whole batch'). Backlog #15 (improvement-backlog.md:569-612) scopes the fix to 'both MhtTracker and PmbmTracker' only. tests/pipeline/test_batch_ordering.cpp instantiates only MhtTracker and PmbmTracker. Integration guide line 353 feeds poll() output to processBatch with no pre-sort.
findings.suggested_fix: Add the same is_sorted fast-path + stable_sort-by-time preamble to Tracker::processBatch (bit-identical for already-sorted input, matching the other two trackers), and stamp noteObservation with the matched measurement's time; alternatively, if the same-timestamp contract for plain Tracker is intentional, make it loud (assert/drop-with-diagnostic on mixed timestamps) and fix Tracker.hpp + the integration guide recipe to say batches must be pre-sorted for this tracker only.

units.coverage_notes: UNIT lens-determinism, scope core/ adapters/ app/ sim/. Method: grepped the full scope for every determinism hazard class, then read each hit in context and traced whether it can reach output; additionally read the hot-path orchestrators end-to-end. FILES READ END-TO-END: core/pipeline/{Tracker,ReorderBuffer,MhtTracker}.cpp+hpp, core/tracking/{TrackManager,TrackTree}.cpp+hpp, core/association/{GnnAssociator,JpdaAssociator,Murty,Hungarian}.cpp, core/estimation/{ParticleFilterEstimator,Resampling}.cpp+hpp, core/collision/CpaEvaluator.cpp+hpp, core/scenario/{Harness,HarnessBatched,Metrics}.cpp, sim/{AisEmitter,SimulatedSensorBus,SkewInjector}.cpp, core/types/{Track,Timestamp}.hpp; read in relevant part: core/pmbm/PmbmTracker.cpp (processBatch, pruneAndNormalise, mergeBernoulliDuplicates, source-touch/clutter-feed/diag sections), PmbmTypes.hpp, core/benchmark/{MarkdownRenderer,Metrics}.cpp, core/bias/{SensorBiasEstimator.hpp,SensorBiasPairExtractor.cpp}. VERIFIED CLEAN under the invariant-#4 contract (same log, same binary → identical output): (1) unordered_map/unordered_set — every instance is either lookup-only (TrackManager::id_to_index_ with the vector driving iteration; SensorBiasEstimator pos_/brg_; sim emitters' truths/next_emit_ which iterate cfg_.targets vector; McapWriter/FoxgloveDebugRecorder; PlotCsvReplayAdapter StationMap) or paired with an explicit first-seen-order vector (MarkdownRenderer, Comparator) or feeds keyed writes only (PMBM staged maps merge into std::map). (2) RNG — no random_device/rand()/time()/clock anywhere in scope; every engine is fixed-seeded (ParticleFilterEstimator ctor seed with a documented draw-discipline; sim emitters seeded via seed_seq(cfg.seed, emitter-id chars); scenario Builders per-function mt19937(seed); SkewInjector uses an integer jitter ladder specifically for cross-platform reproducibility). steady_clock appears only in bench latency columns (BenchRunner.cpp:67, Sweep.cpp:292/586) — measurement-only, by design (R2), never feeds tracker state or metric selection. (3) sorts — stable_sort used everywhere ties are reachable on the batch path (MhtTracker:224, PmbmTracker:1331, SimulatedSensorBus:142, SkewInjector:54, Builders:109); unstable std::sort sites (replay/bench loaders, TrackTree::pruneKLocal/mergeBranches score sorts, JPDA overflow fallback, PMBM partial_sorts, mergeBernoulliDuplicates id sort) all have strict-weak comparators on int64/id/double keys and are deterministic for identical input; NaN sort keys would require lambda_C=0 && P_D=0 style configs already covered by B2's clutter_intensity==0 finding. (4) no pointer-keyed containers (the two `const X*` maps are pointer-VALUED, keyed by BernoulliId/SensorBiasKey); no static mutable state in scope; ReorderBuffer's multimap preserves insertion order for equal keys (C++11) so drain order is deterministic; Murty priority_queue and Hungarian tie-breaks are first-wins deterministic; GNN greedy strict-< deterministic and NaN-cost-safe (NaN never beats best). (5) uninitialized-read hunt: TrackTreeNode declares uninitialized members (parent/scan_idx/score/is_leaf) but both construction sites (rootFromMeasurement, TrackTree::branch miss+hit) assign every field; Track/Bernoulli/PoissonComponent/SourceTouch fully default-initialized. OVERFLOW ONE-LINERS (not reported): (a) CpaEvaluator.cpp:67 — Exited events for tracks dropped in the same cycle are emitted in unordered_set iteration order: deterministic per binary/input, but relative event order for simultaneous drops is unspecified across STL implementations (cosmetic; contract is same-binary replay). (b) PmbmTracker compares timestamps via double seconds() in three places (t_max selection :1370, stageActivityCheck :1298, birth-time equality :1608) instead of int64 nanos — deterministic but collapses distinctions below ~238 ns at 2026 epochs; :1608 equality is already filed by B2. (c) Timestamp::fromSeconds round-trips in sim (AisEmitter cadence, SimulatedSensorBus ticks) truncate toward zero, drifting emission instants by sub-µs — deterministic, harmless. (d) bench latency columns make bench CSV/markdown outputs non-reproducible by design; freeze/diff workflows must exclude them (already project practice). DEDUP: I independently found Tracker::process()+JPDA silent duplicate-birth before checking prior outputs; it is already CONFIRMED in 10-bughunt-findings.md so not re-reported; also cross-checked my finding list against all five raw unit files (B1-B5) — my single reported finding (Tracker::processBatch missing the #15 sort) appears nowhere there (B2 covered the PMBM-side mixed-timestamp double-propagation and the PMBM float-equality birth gate, not the plain Tracker's missing sort/whole-batch drop). NOT COVERED by me in depth: estimator math internals (B1), PMBM enumerateChildren cost construction (B2), geo/bias math (B3), collision/static/adapter validation (B4), replay loaders/metrics math (B5) — I only re-checked those files' ordering/RNG/uninit surfaces for the determinism lens.

findings.title: Heading-bias observation families disagree in sign (marine vs ENU-math frame): v1/v2 sources measure -b while v3 measures +b, and the documented adapter correction DOUBLES the bearing error for v1/v2
findings.description: The scalar bias state b is consumed by ArpaAdapter/EoIrAdapter as a MARINE-frame gyro bias: they compose marine bearing = relative + pose.heading_true_deg and then subtract b_hat (ArpaAdapter.cpp:119 'bearing_true_rad_corrected = bearing_true_rad - b_hat'; EoIrAdapter.cpp:45 same). The v3 gyro-vs-X observations feed exactly that quantity: measurement = wrapToPi(gyro_rad - gps_true_heading_rad) = +b_marine (HeadingBiasEstimator.cpp:157/177/193). But the v1 AIS-ARPA pair observation computes the disagreement in the ENU MATH frame: z = wrapToPi(beta_arpa - beta_truth) with beta = atan2(y,x) (HeadingBiasEstimator.cpp:43-46), and the v2 bearing-innovation is likewise math-frame: r = wrapAngle(beta_obs - beta_pred) (Tracker.cpp:39, model bearing = atan2(dy,dx) per MeasurementModels.cpp:30). Because math angle = pi/2 - marine angle, a physical marine gyro bias +b appears as -b in any math-frame bearing difference: a target at marine bearing theta projected via x=r*sin(theta+b), y=r*cos(theta+b) (Projection.cpp:18-19) has math angle atan2(y,x) = pi/2-(theta+b) = math_true - b, so z = -b and r = -b. All five kinds update the SAME state b (scalar KF), so (1) mixing v1/v2 with v3 — exactly the wiring docs/integration-guide.md section 5 and tests/integration/test_full_stack_pipeline.cpp prescribe (setHeadingBiasEstimator + setBearingInnovationSink on one estimator, then pass it to ArpaAdapter/EoIrAdapter) — fuses contradictory measurements +b and -b into one state; and (2) with v1/v2 sources alone, b_hat converges to -b and the adapters' subtraction yields theta + b - (-b) = theta + 2b: the 'correction' doubles the bearing error instead of removing it, then closed-loop feedback (z becomes b_hat - b, innovation y = -b every cycle) ratchets b_hat further negative each accepted update, rotating every radar/EO-IR measurement progressively farther from truth. Contrast: the per-sensor SensorBiasEstimator loop is frame-consistent because it both measures AND applies its bearing bias in the math frame on the Bearing2D value (SensorBiasEstimator.cpp:142-143, BiasCorrection.hpp:72). Why tests do not catch it: tests/bias/test_heading_bias_estimator.cpp builds pairs directly in math angles (ARPA at beta_truth + bias, line ~23), i.e. it pins the estimator's internal convention, not the physics of a marine gyro bias flowing through the adapters; tests/scenario/test_bearing_bias_convergence.cpp likewise injects the bias directly on math-frame Bearing2D values; tests/integration/test_full_stack_pipeline.cpp injects the +2deg gyro bias only into the self-consistent v3 sources (target measurements are AIS-style Position2D, so the wired v2 sink never sees a biased bearing); and the only end-to-end closed-loop tests through the real adapters (tests/sim/test_bus_bias_estimator_sweep.cpp) run with knob.bias_deg = 0 and end in SUCCEED() with no assertions.
findings.failure_scenario: Own-ship gyro reads 10 deg high (b = +10 deg, HDT-fed pose.heading_true_deg = true + 10). Radar target at true marine bearing 30 deg, range 1500 m. ArpaAdapter composes 40 deg and projects the target ~260 m clockwise of truth. AIS-ARPA pairs give beta_arpa = 50 deg math vs beta_truth = 60 deg math, z = -10 deg; after ~10 pairs p_b < (0.3 deg)^2 and the estimator publishes b_hat ~= -10 deg. From the next TTM on, ArpaAdapter computes corrected bearing = 40 - (-10) = 50 deg: the measurement is now ~520 m from truth — twice the uncorrected error — and every subsequent accepted pair pushes b_hat further negative (innovation stays -b), so radar and EO-IR measurements rotate steadily away from truth until gating/association breaks, all silently. If the operator additionally wires the v3 sources per the integration guide, v3 pushes b_hat toward +10 deg while v1/v2 push toward -10 deg, leaving a wrong, oscillating estimate that corrupts bearings in whichever direction currently dominates.
findings.evidence: HeadingBiasEstimator.cpp:43-46: 'const double beta_truth = std::atan2(ais_rel.y(), ais_rel.x()); const double beta_arpa = std::atan2(arpa_rel.y(), arpa_rel.x()); const double z = wrapToPi(beta_arpa - beta_truth);' — math-frame difference. ArpaAdapter.cpp:106-119: 'if (bearing_units == "R") bearing_true_deg += own_opt->heading_true_deg; ... const double bearing_true_rad_corrected = bearing_true_rad - b_hat;' — marine-frame subtraction. Projection.cpp:18-19: 'out.pos_enu.x() = own + range_m * sb; out.pos_enu.y() = own + range_m * cb;' — marine bearing convention, so math angle of the projected point = pi/2 - marine. OwnShipNmeaAdapter.cpp:234/259: 'obs.gps_cog_rad = wrapDegToPi(cog_deg)' fed as measurement = wrapToPi(gyro - cog) = +b_marine (HeadingBiasEstimator.cpp:177). Worked numbers: marine 30deg -> unit (sin30,cos30)=(0.5,0.866) -> atan2(0.866,0.5)=60deg math; with b=+10: marine 40 -> math 50; z = 50-60 = -10 = -b; corrected = 40-(-10) = 50 vs truth 30 (error doubled).
findings.suggested_fix: Pick ONE definition of b (the natural one is the marine gyro bias b = gyro - truth that v3 already measures and the adapters already subtract) and fix the two math-frame sources to it: v1: z = wrapToPi(beta_truth - beta_arpa); v2: feed obs.innovation_rad negated when treated as a measurement of b (or emit r = wrapAngle(beta_pred - beta_obs) for the bias sink specifically, keeping the general IInnovationSink as-is). Then add the missing end-to-end pin: a sim-bus test with own_cfg.heading_bias_deg != 0 that asserts (a) b_hat converges to +bias_deg in radians and (b) corrected ARPA measurement error < uncorrected error, so the frame contract can never silently flip again.

units.coverage_notes: Scope: units/angles/frames lens over ALL of core/, adapters/, app/, sim/ (tests read only to check pinning). I first read docs/reviews/2026-07-09-prerelease-review/10-bughunt-findings.md and excluded everything already reported there (incl. TrackOutput NED ordering + transposed meridian rotation, Cpa tcpa Jacobian sign, datumAxisRotation antimeridian, DatumShift rotation sign, DeclaredSensorActivity coverage-from-origin, AisCsvReplayAdapter knots-as-m/s, UKF sigma-point bearing mean across +-pi, RangeBearing2D-as-ENU initiate bugs, ArpaAdapter missing sensor_position_enu for v1 pairs, closed-loop double-subtract 'half convergence' — note my finding shows that analysis was itself frame-inverted: the loop diverges rather than halving, wrapDegToPi Inf hang, GGA/HDT Null Island, example.cpp port/starboard comment, sim TTM 14-field shift, OwnShipEmitter stddev-0). Verified clean (hand-executed the math): Wgs84 geodetic<->ECEF (Bowring) and deg/rad; Datum ENU rotation matrix rows; Projection.cpp polar->ENU Jacobian (compass convention, J=[[sb,r*cb],[cb,-r*sb]] correct); PolarVelocity sogCogToEnuVelocity (marine COG -> ENU, Jacobian + iso floor); TrackOutput COG = atan2(e,n), [0,360) normalization, SOG/COG polar Jacobian rows, near-zero-SOG fallback; MeasurementModels wrapAngle (fmod-based, returns (-pi,pi], no loop hang) and residual wrapping applied at ALL residual call sites (EkfEstimator:41,154, Gating:11, MhtTracker:580, PF:87, UKF zd:86-87, IMM zd:215-216); ClutterMapDetectionModel azimuth cell indexing/wrap (remainder-based) and bilinear position interpolation; BearingWedgeModel angleDelta + math->true deg output conversion (90-alpha, mod 360); ShadowMask unwrap-at-largest-gap logic; CameraBearingCsvReader marine->math (pi/2 - theta, wrapped); AutoferryJsonReplay NED->ENU bearing (pi/2 - b_ned) and (E,N) assembly; PlotCsvReplayAdapter/HaxrTruthLoader/RadarTruthCsvReader marine sin/cos projections; AisAdapter sentinels (lat 91/lon 181, SOG 1023, COG >=360, heading 511, nav_status 15) and knots factor 0.514444; ArpaAdapter TTM field indexing vs NMEA layout (bearing[2], T/R[3], speed[4], course[5], T/R[6], shared units[9]), distance/speed unit tables (N/K/S), TLL/GGA ddmm parsing incl. size_t underflow guard; OwnShipNmeaAdapter RMC SOG knots->m/s, COG->ENU, HDG deviation/variation signs (E+/W-), gyro-rate wrap; NmeaEncode ddmm formatting with 60-minute carry; sim AisEmitter cadence knots buckets, ArpaEmitter/EoIrEmitter compass atan2(E,N) conventions consistent with heading=0 deferral, OwnShipEmitter RMC/HDT encoding + heading normalization; TruthTrajectory constant-turn closed-form integration; CoordinatedTurn/CV5State F and Q dt-scaling (CT velocity rotation is proper CCW ENU); SourceTouchPopulate RangeBearing2D math-frame (r*cos,r*sin) consistent with its atan2(dy,dx) model; BiasCorrection.hpp per-sensor bearing bias measured AND applied in the math frame (consistent, unlike the heading-bias loop I reported); GeoJSON [lon,lat] ordering in both land and static parsers; CoastlineGeometry equirectangular m-per-deg; SensorDefaults deg->rad; NavInputGuard heading delta wrap [0,180]; OwnShipProvider sorted insert/recenter threshold km->m; RemoteTrackAdapter (velocity already ENU, no conversion); CpaOwnShip synthesis; OwnShipVelocityEstimator LS fit units. Overflow one-liners (below reporting bar): (1) ArpaAdapter.cpp:42 assumes m/s for an unlabelled TTM units field — m/s is not an NMEA TTM unit; a malformed sentence yields ~2x speed error in the birth-velocity hint only; (2) Wgs84.cpp:46 ecefToGeodetic divides by cos(lat) — degenerate exactly at the poles (irrelevant for maritime datums); (3) EoIrAdapter never sets Measurement.sensor_position_enu (stays (0,0)) — same family as the already-reported ArpaAdapter omission; consumed by PMBM range-conditioned paramsForAt (PmbmTracker.cpp:646), so EO-IR coverage conditioning is origin-relative when own-ship is far from the datum; (4) sweep tests test_bus_bias_estimator_sweep.cpp are assertion-free (SUCCEED() after printing) and run with bias_deg=0 — the heading-bias closed loop has no end-to-end pin at all (folded into the main finding's fix). Not audited: tests/ internals beyond pin-checking, gitignored fixtures/data (per brief), docs-only files, core/benchmark CSV/markdown plumbing beyond unit greps (prior batch B5 covered it).
